name: Salmon & Shiitakes Steamed in Romaine
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Bottled hot or regular Teriyaki sauce
1/4 lb Shiitake mushrooms, stems trimmed and wiped clean, thinly sliced (about 1/2 inch)
8 large Romaine leaves, cleaned with the thicker part of the rib removed
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the marinated salmon: Cut salmon fillets crosswise into 4 pieces and place in a glass pan. Combine 2 tablespoons teriyaki sauce, lemon juice, and mint. Pour over the salmon and refrigerate for one hour.
2. Prepare the vegetables: Heat oil in a nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add mushrooms and ginger; sauté until softened (about four minutes). Add mint and 1 tablespoon teriyaki sauce; bring to a boil for 1 minute. Remove from pan.
3. Prepare the steaming liquid: Combine water, mint, lemon slices, and ginger in a large pot; bring to a low boil. Reduce heat slightly so the liquid remains at a low boil. Place a steamer basket or rack over the pot and lay Romaine leaves on top. Cover and steam for 4 minutes per inch of thickness until wilted. Remove lettuce from steamer.
4. Remove salmon from marinade (discard marinade). Spoon mushrooms over each piece of salmon and wrap with 2 steamed Romaine leaves. Place in steamer basket and steam for twelve minutes per inch of thickness, testing the salmon in center for doneness.
5. Prepare sauce: Stir together teriyaki sauce, lemon juice, sugar, and mint. Serve on side of salmon.
